focus: Zambia tax-benefit surface: national income tax (PAYE), rental income tax, presumptive small-business tax, NSSF contributions, Local Service Tax, VAT, excise duties, and the Senior Citizens Grant.
purpose: Household-level comparison-oracle and supporting-source references for Zambia Axiom coverage. None of these are legal authority.
oracle_scope: A RuleSpec oracle must be an executable, pinned external calculator that accepts household-level inputs and returns household-level tax-benefit outputs comparable to Axiom outputs. Aggregate simulators, distributional reports, parameter documentation, and public model summaries are supporting references only, not oracles.

oracles.name: MicroZAMOD
oracles.dataset: ug_2016_a11.txt, ug_2020_a1.txt, ug_2024_a1.txt (UNHS-based input microdata shipped in the bundle)
oracles.role: Household-level tax-benefit comparison oracle for Zambia. MicroZAMOD is the SOUTHMOD tax-benefit microsimulation model for Zambia (UNU-WIDER, in collaboration with the Ministry of Finance, Planning and Economic Development). Built on the EUROMOD platform; systems UG_2016-UG_2025.

availability_check.detail: The licensed SOUTHMOD A4.0 bundle held under the Adhesion Agreement for the Ghana lane also carries MicroZAMOD with systems UG_2016-UG_2025 and three bundled UNHS-based input datasets, so per-case and dataset-backed probes need no further data application (personal links; non-redistributable; the bundle and datasets must never be committed or shared). The EUROMOD-engine runtime is proven from the Ghana lane (x86_64 py3.12 venv + euromod 0.2.18 + x64 .NET 10). Oracle wiring (zm: prefix mapping, UG country scope, per-case parity suites against UG_2025 = FY2025/26) follows the Ghana recipe; until wired, zm: outputs remain classified not_comparable and parity tests stay deferred. The UG_2025 policy-surface inventory (23 policies, 9 active instruments) is recorded at ~/.axiom/oracles/southmod/harness-zm/ugamod/inventory_result.json.
